canonical_name: Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia
description: Colombian narcoparamilitary organization and one of the emerging criminal gangs serving drug trafficking (Bacrim), part of the Colombian internal armed conflict. Founded in 2006 by Pedro Oliviero Guerrero Castillo alias 'Cuchillo' with 200 men, allied with drug trafficker Daniel Barrera alias 'El Loco Barrera'. After Cuchillo's death and Barrera's capture, 500 members surrendered in 2011, and the group split into two blocs. As of 2016, each bloc had 100 or fewer combatants.

country_links.evidence_quote: promotor de rutas para el narcotráfico en Venezuela y Brasil
country_links.context: The ERPAC promoted drug trafficking routes through Brazil.
